title: Ericoides tetralix

Within the Cyperaceae family, it is classified as a member of the tribe Schoeneae. The tribe Schoeneae is further divided into several genera, including Ericoides. These classifications are based on morphological and phylogenetic characteristics of the plant.

intro: Ericoides tetralix, commonly known as the Buttonbush, is a flowering plant native to North America, characterized by its spherical clusters of small flowers and distinctive shape. The Buttonbush has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, providing relief from respiratory issues such as bronchitis, asthma, and coughs due to its anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties.
